title: empty-map guard lost its why-we-fail-loud rationale
description: The commit removed the second sentence of the comment above the is_empty() guard: "Returning \"{}\" as a valid secret would silently give callers an empty value; fail loudly instead." What remains only states the observable fact ("KV secret was written with no fields"), not the design decision (return an error rather than returning "{}"). A contributor who sees the error will not understand why we do not fall through to the to_string serialization path. Suggested restore: add back the rationale sentence.
